codeReview.summary: Implementation is correct and complete. FormatSelector component uses pill-style buttons with single-select behavior, visual highlighting with input-type-specific colors (blue for video, purple for audio, green for image), and formats adapt correctly based on input type via getAvailableFormats(). Integration with App.tsx properly wires onFormatSelect to electronAPI.updateItemSettings(). No unit tests were added, but the skill procedure for format-selector-component does not explicitly require them (unlike unit-tests-m1 which has a separate test creation procedure).

issues.description: handleFormatClick has unused parameter 'e' of type MouseEvent<HTMLButtonElement>. Only e.preventDefault() and e.stopPropagation() are used. Could simplify to remove unused parameter or prefix with underscore.

CSS module class name lookup styles[inputTypeClass] depends on dynamic string values from getInputTypeClass(). This works correctly since the function returns 'video-format', 'audio-format', or 'image-format' which match CSS class names, but it's a minor style concern. Could be more explicit.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill lists 'agent-browser' as a Required Skill for manual verification, but the transcript shows the worker did not invoke it. The worker verified only through automated checks (typecheck, test, build). The skill procedure says 'Verify' for format-selector-component but doesn't explicitly require interactive browser testing, so this is a deviation but not necessarily a failure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton shows only npm run typecheck, npm run test, npm run build were run. No agent-browser invocation.

summary: FormatSelector component implementation is complete and correct. Pill-style buttons, single-select behavior, and visual highlighting with input-type-specific colors are all properly implemented. Formats correctly adapt based on input type using getAvailableFormats(). Integration with App.tsx and electronAPI is properly wired. Two minor code style issues noted (unused parameter, dynamic CSS class lookup) but neither is blocking.
